heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Billy Wilder. Written by Walter Newman, Lesser Samuels, and Billy Wilder.
clean: Directed by Billy Wilder. Written by Walter Newman, Lesser Samuels, and Billy Wilder.

heading: Charles Tatum
raw: Bad news sells best. 'Cause good news is no news.
clean: Bad news sells best. 'Cause good news is no news.

heading: Charles Tatum
raw: I've done a lot of lying in my time. I've lied to men who wear belts. I've lied to men who wear suspenders. But I'd never be so stupid as to lie to a man who wears both belt and suspenders.
clean: I've done a lot of lying in my time. I've lied to men who wear belts. I've lied to men who wear suspenders. But I'd never be so stupid as to lie to a man who wears both belt and suspenders.

heading: Charles Tatum
raw: It's a good story today. Tomorrow, it'll be yesterday's news and they'll wrap a fish in it.
clean: It's a good story today. Tomorrow, it'll be yesterday's news and they'll wrap a fish in it.

heading: Charles Tatum
raw: I can handle big news and little news. And if there's no news, I'll go out and bite a dog.
clean: I can handle big news and little news. And if there's no news, I'll go out and bite a dog.

heading: Charles Tatum
raw: When they bleached your hair, they must have bleached your brain too.
clean: When they bleached your hair, they must have bleached your brain too.

heading: Charles Tatum
raw: You know what's wrong with New Mexico, Mr. Wendel? Too much outdoors.
clean: You know what's wrong with New Mexico, Mr. Wendel? Too much outdoors.

heading: Charles Tatum
raw: How'd you like to make a thousand dollars a day, Mr. Boot? I'm a thousand-dollar-a-day newspaperman. You can have me for nothing.
clean: How'd you like to make a thousand dollars a day, Mr. Boot? I'm a thousand-dollar-a-day newspaperman. You can have me for nothing.

heading: Lorraine Minosa
raw: I don't go to church. Kneeling bags my nylons.
clean: I don't go to church. Kneeling bags my nylons.

heading: Lorraine Minosa
raw: I've met a lot of hard-boiled eggs in my time, but you--you're twenty minutes.
clean: I've met a lot of hard-boiled eggs in my time, but you--you're twenty minutes.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reporter: We're all in the same boat here.
clean: We're all in the same boat here.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: I'm in the boat. You're in the water. Now let's see how you can swim.
clean: I'm in the boat. You're in the water. Now let's see how you can swim.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: Mr. Boot, I was passing through Albuquerque; had breakfast here. I read your paper and thought you might be interested in my reaction.
clean: Mr. Boot, I was passing through Albuquerque; had breakfast here. I read your paper and thought you might be interested in my reaction.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jacob Q. Boot: Indeed I am.
clean: Indeed I am.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: Well, to be honest, it made me throw up. I don't mean to tell you I was expecting the New York Times, but even for Albuquerque, this is pretty Albuquerque.
clean: Well, to be honest, it made me throw up. I don't mean to tell you I was expecting the New York Times, but even for Albuquerque, this is pretty Albuquerque.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jacob Q. Boot: Alright, here's your nickel back.
clean: Alright, here's your nickel back.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: Where's my desk?
clean: Where's my desk?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jacob Q. Boot: The one by the door. You may be out of here by Saturday.
clean: The one by the door. You may be out of here by Saturday.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: Sooner the better.
clean: Sooner the better.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Deputy Sheriff: Hey you! Sheriff wants to see you, down at the trading post. Don't waste any of his time 'cause he ain't staying around long. Maybe you ain't either.
clean: Hey you! Sheriff wants to see you, down at the trading post. Don't waste any of his time 'cause he ain't staying around long. Maybe you ain't either.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: You don't say?
clean: You don't say?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Deputy Sheriff: You wanna know something? He don't like you.
clean: You wanna know something? He don't like you.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: And I was going to propose to him.
clean: And I was going to propose to him.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sheriff: You're that Tatum guy that was popping off over the phone last night!
clean: You're that Tatum guy that was popping off over the phone last night!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: I wasn't popping off, Sheriff; I was threatening. "Play along with me and you'll get re-elected. Don't, and I'll crucify you," that's what I said, remember?
clean: I wasn't popping off, Sheriff; I was threatening. "Play along with me and you'll get re-elected. Don't, and I'll crucify you," that's what I said, remember?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sheriff: I think I'll have my boys take you down to the county line and throw you out!
clean: I think I'll have my boys take you down to the county line and throw you out!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: Throw out your campaign manager? You need plenty of help.
clean: Throw out your campaign manager? You need plenty of help.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sheriff: And maybe before I throw you out, I'll toss you into the 'Klink' for awhile!
clean: And maybe before I throw you out, I'll toss you into the 'Klink' for awhile!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: I don't belong in your office. Not with that embroidered sign on the wall; it gets in my way.
clean: I don't belong in your office. Not with that embroidered sign on the wall; it gets in my way.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jacob Q. Boot: Then it does bother you a little.
clean: Then it does bother you a little.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: Not enough to stop me. I'm on my way back to the top, and if it takes a deal with a crooked sheriff, that's alright with me! And if I have to fancy it up with an Indian curse and a broken hearted wife for Leo, then that's alright too!
clean: Not enough to stop me. I'm on my way back to the top, and if it takes a deal with a crooked sheriff, that's alright with me! And if I have to fancy it up with an Indian curse and a broken hearted wife for Leo, then that's alright too!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jacob Q. Boot: Do you drink a lot?
clean: Do you drink a lot?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: Not a lot - just frequently.
clean: Not a lot - just frequently.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: When the history of this sunbaked Siberia is written, these shameful words will live in infamy: 'No chopped chicken liver.' No garlic pickles. No Lindy's. No Madison Square Garden. No Yogi Berra. What do you know about Yogi Berra, Miss Deverich?
clean: When the history of this sunbaked Siberia is written, these shameful words will live in infamy: 'No chopped chicken liver.' No garlic pickles. No Lindy's. No Madison Square Garden. No Yogi Berra. What do you know about Yogi Berra, Miss Deverich?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Miss Deverich: Yogi? Why, it's a sort of religion, isn't it?
clean: Yogi? Why, it's a sort of religion, isn't it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Miss Deverich: Oh, Mr. Tatum. Really.
clean: Oh, Mr. Tatum. Really.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: Or you, Mr. Wendell. If you'd only toss that cigar out of the window - real far, all the way to Los Alamos - And boom!! [He chuckles] Now there would be a story.
clean: Or you, Mr. Wendell. If you'd only toss that cigar out of the window - real far, all the way to Los Alamos - And boom!! Now there would be a story.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Herbie Cook: [Looking at the unstable cliff-dwelling] I don't like the looks of it, Chuck.
clean: I don't like the looks of it, Chuck.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: Neither do I, fan, but I like the odds.
clean: Neither do I, fan, but I like the odds.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Herbie Cook: The old man sure looked bad. Did you see his face?
clean: The old man sure looked bad. Did you see his face?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: Yeah.
clean: Yeah.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Herbie Cook: Like the faces of those folks you see outside a coal mine with maybe 84 men trapped inside.
clean: Like the faces of those folks you see outside a coal mine with maybe 84 men trapped inside.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: One man's better than 84. Didn't they teach you that?
clean: One man's better than 84. Didn't they teach you that?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Herbie Cook: Teach me what?
clean: Teach me what?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Charles Tatum: Human interest. You pick up the paper, you read about 84 men or 284, or a million men, like in a Chinese famine. You read it, but it doesn't stay with you. One man's different, you want to know all about him. That's human interest.
clean: Human interest. You pick up the paper, you read about 84 men or 284, or a million men, like in a Chinese famine. You read it, but it doesn't stay with you. One man's different, you want to know all about him. That's human interest.
